With Compassion

Dr. Hina Ahmed brings 15+ years of specialized neurology experience to provide personalized, evidence-based care for complex neurological conditions. Your brain health deserves expertise you can trust.

Patient Satisfaction

4.9/5 Stars

About Dr. Hina

Dedicated to Advancing
Neurological Wellness

Dr. Hina Ahmed combines world-class medical training with a genuine passion for patient advocacy, delivering neurological care that's both scientifically rigorous and deeply compassionate.

Medical Education

MD, Neurology Residency - Johns Hopkins Hospital

Board Certification

American Board of Psychiatry & Neurology

Fellowship Training

Clinical Neurophysiology & Epilepsy - Mayo Clinic

Professional Memberships

AAN, AES, CNS, ANA - Active Member & Committee Contributor

Philosophy of Care

Neurological conditions affect not just the nervous system, but every aspect of a person's life - their work, relationships, independence, and sense of self. My approach centers on treating the whole person, not just the diagnosis.

I believe in the power of listening. Every patient's story holds clues that no test can reveal. By combining thorough clinical evaluation with advanced diagnostics and the latest evidence-based treatments, I create personalized care plans that address both immediate symptoms and long-term neurological health.

"Expertise without empathy is incomplete. Compassion without competence is insufficient. My mission is to provide both."

Education & Training Timeline

2020 - Present

Attending Neurologist, Dr. Hina Neuro

Private practice focusing on complex neurological disorders

2015 - 2020

Staff Neurologist, Regional Medical Center

Led epilepsy monitoring unit & neurology consultation service

2013 - 2015

Clinical Neurophysiology Fellowship, Mayo Clinic

Advanced EEG, EMG, evoked potentials, intraoperative monitoring

2009 - 2013

Neurology Residency, Johns Hopkins Hospital

Chief Resident, final year. Research: Neuroinflammation in MS

Areas of Expertise

Specialized Neurological Care
Across the Spectrum

From common headaches to complex movement disorders, Dr. Hina provides comprehensive diagnosis and treatment for the full range of neurological conditions.

Epilepsy & Seizure Disorders

Advanced diagnostic evaluation, medication management, vagus nerve stimulation, and surgical referral for refractory epilepsy.

Focal SeizuresGeneralized EpilepsyStatus EpilepticusEpilepsy in Pregnancy

Headache & Migraine

Comprehensive headache management including Botox for chronic migraine, CGRP inhibitors, and personalized prevention strategies.

Chronic MigraineCluster HeadacheTension HeadacheMedication Overuse

Movement Disorders

Expert care for Parkinson's disease, essential tremor, dystonia, and other movement disorders including deep brain stimulation programming.

Parkinson's DiseaseEssential TremorDystoniaAtaxia

Multiple Sclerosis & Neuroimmunology

Disease-modifying therapy selection, relapse management, and comprehensive MS care following latest treatment algorithms.

Relapsing MSProgressive MSNMOSDMOGAD

Neuromuscular Disorders

Diagnosis and management of peripheral neuropathies, myopathies, and neuromuscular junction disorders.

Peripheral NeuropathyMyasthenia GravisALSGuillain-Barre

Clinical Neurophysiology

Advanced electrodiagnostic testing including EEG, EMG/NCS, evoked potentials, and intraoperative monitoring.

Video EEGEMG/NCSVEP/BAER/SEPIOM
Why Choose Us

Your Neurological Health
Deserves the Best

We combine world-class expertise with personalized attention to deliver outcomes that matter.

Patient-Centered Care

Every treatment plan is tailored to your unique needs, lifestyle, and goals. You're not a case number - you're a person.

Academic Excellence

Trained at Johns Hopkins and Mayo Clinic. Board-certified with active research contributions and leadership in national neurology organizations.

Cutting-Edge Technology

State-of-the-art diagnostics including 3T MRI, video-EEG monitoring, and advanced neurophysiology testing on-site.

Collaborative Approach

We coordinate seamlessly with your primary care, physical therapy, neurosurgery, and other specialists for comprehensive care.

Safety First

Evidence-based protocols, regular outcome tracking, and transparent communication about risks and benefits of every treatment option.

Accessible & Responsive

Same-week appointments for new patients. Direct portal messaging. After-hours coverage for urgent neurological concerns.

Your Path to Wellness

A Streamlined Process
Designed Around You

We've designed a patient journey that makes neurological care as smooth and effective as possible.

01

Comprehensive Evaluation

90-minute initial consultation including detailed history, neurological examination, and review of prior records.

02

Advanced Diagnostics

On-site MRI, EEG, EMG/NCS, and evoked potentials. Results often available same day.

03

Personalized Treatment

Evidence-based treatment plan with clear goals, timeline, and follow-up schedule. Medication, therapy, or surgical referral as needed.

04

Ongoing Partnership

Regular follow-ups, remote monitoring, direct messaging access. We adjust treatment as your needs evolve.

Our Services

Comprehensive Neurological
Services

Full spectrum of diagnostic and therapeutic services for neurological conditions.

EEG & Video-EEG Monitoring

Routine, sleep-deprived, ambulatory, and long-term video-EEG for seizure characterization.

EMG/Nerve Conduction Studies

Comprehensive electrodiagnostic testing for neuropathy, radiculopathy, and neuromuscular disorders.

Evoked Potentials (VEP/BAER/SEP)

Visual, brainstem auditory, and somatosensory evoked potentials for demyelinating and compressive lesions.

Botox for Chronic Migraine

FDA-approved onabotulinumtoxinA injections for chronic migraine prophylaxis per PREEMPT protocol.

CGRP Inhibitor Therapy

Latest monoclonal antibody and gepant treatments for migraine prevention and acute treatment.

Deep Brain Stimulation Management

DBS programming and optimization for Parkinson's disease, essential tremor, and dystonia.

Infusion Therapy

In-office IVIG, natalizumab, ocrelizumab, and other disease-modifying therapy infusions.

Telemedicine Visits

Secure video consultations for follow-ups, medication management, and acute concerns.

Conditions We Treat

Expertise Across the
Neurological Spectrum

From the most common to the most complex neurological disorders.

Epilepsy & Seizures
Migraine & Chronic Headache
Parkinson's Disease
Essential Tremor
Dystonia
Multiple Sclerosis
Peripheral Neuropathy
Myasthenia Gravis
ALS
Guillain-Barre Syndrome
CIDP
Ataxia
Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus
Memory Disorders
Concussion & TBI
Sleep Disorders
Frequently Asked Questions

Your Questions,
Answered

Common questions about our practice and neurological care.

We accept both self-referrals and physician referrals. Some insurance plans may require a referral, so we recommend checking with your insurance provider. Our team can help verify your benefits before your appointment.

Please bring your insurance card, photo ID, list of current medications, any prior imaging (MRI/CT) on CD, previous EEG/EMG reports, and a list of questions you'd like to discuss. A detailed symptom timeline is also very helpful.

New patient consultations are 90 minutes to allow for a comprehensive history, neurological examination, and development of a personalized care plan. Follow-up visits are typically 30 minutes.

Yes, Dr. Hina performs Botox injections for chronic migraine per the PREEMPT protocol, as well as trigger point injections and nerve blocks for headache and facial pain disorders.

We accept most major insurance plans including Medicare, Blue Cross Blue Shield, Aetna, Cigna, UnitedHealthcare, and others. Our team verifies your benefits and provides a cost estimate before your visit.

Yes, we offer secure video visits for follow-up appointments, medication management, and acute concerns. In-person visits are recommended for new patients and those requiring neurological examinations.
